rulings.Case Title: KALLE KARL-HEINZ PIEPER ET AL VS. 640 OCTAVIA, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 22, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is OFF CALENDAR. The only documents on file in support of this motion are a "re-notice" and 3 volumes of exhibits. The only courtesy copy received from the moving party is the "re-notice" (no exhibits and no replies). The filing does not contain Points and Authorities or the motion. The Court notes that there are three separate oppositions to this one notice of motion and three separate replies. If defendants choose to file a Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings, there must be one opposition and one reply to one motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARLENE RAMOS ET AL VS. JAY POINDEXTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 22, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT EDUART HOXHA'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is GRANTED. Plaintiff must serve a complete copy of all required documents (including the ADR package) within 60 days of this ruling. If Plaintiff fails to effectuate proper service within 60 days, Plaintiff must comply with the requirements of CRC 3.110. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: LINDA SU VS. LOUISE TAYLOR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 22, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer: tenant is guilty of unlawful detainer when the tenant continued in possession by subtenant. CCP 1161(1). Complaint alleges that tenant continues in possession via Mock and failed to vacate per the terms of the Lease. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
